title: Ceiling of the Sistine Chapel
description: This quiz is for the video Michelangelo, ceiling of the Sistine Chapel.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Michelangelo brought a strong [[☃ dropdown 1]] element to the painted figures on the Sistine Chapel.**

![Detail from Michelangelo’s Sistine Chapel ceiling showing Adam reaching out his hand toward God in The Creation of Adam.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/078c81c949c085ac6fe488e43bff20ef.png)
hints: **Michelangelo brought a strong sculptural element to the painted figures on the Sistine Chapel.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of Michelangelo's ceiling for the Sistine Chapel?**

![Ceiling fresco of the Sistine Chapel by Michelangelo, depicting biblical scenes with numerous figures framed by architectural details.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/39dcb3db6b478b09ef5738c3498fa145.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The architectural setting that surrounds the central scenes is an illusion, created with paint.**

choices.content: The scenes along the central panel represent episodes from Genesis in the Old Testament.
The outer scenes show the prophets and sibyls that foretell the coming of Christ.
The scenes from the central panel show the Temptation of Adam and Eve and their Expulsion from Eden indicating the need for Christ's sacrifice in the New Testament.
The architectural framing that we see surrounding the central scenes is the only real element on the ceiling, the remainder is paint.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Michelangelo's figures recall the strength, beauty, and idealism of  classical sculpture.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fc284247df48b9cd1ef17d5687e2deeb.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Michelangelo's sculptural figures recall the strength, beauty, and idealism of classical sculpture.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of the sibyls?**

![Michelangelo’s Sistine Chapel fresco of the Delphic Sibyl, a seated female figure in a golden-orange robe holding a scroll.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/72734e7a999dd5c87cfe159eff0774ab.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Correct answer:   
The body of the Libyan Sybil twists in a complex pose that is typical of the High Renaissance.

choices.content: The sibyls were ancient pagan soothsayers who could foresee the future and in the Catholic tradition, they foretold the coming of Christ.
Michelangelo used a male model for the Libyan sybil, which may explain the great physical strength we see in her body.
The body of the Libyan Sybil twists in a complex pose that is typical of the High Renaissance.
